title: Vectors, Matrices, Transformations, and Data Systems · Quantity Clinic: Inverse Conditioning
summary: Represent flows and transformations across a small service system. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int inverse conditioning increment in a matrix-based flow and transformation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Solve invertible systems and distinguish existence from numerical stability.
Interpret matrices as transformations of vectors and coordinate systems.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Inverse Conditioning evidence
steps.instruction: Calculate the determinant of [[5, 1], [2, 8]]. Use the result to decide Inverse Conditioning for a matrix-based flow and transformation model.
steps.why: The Inverse Conditioning result for a matrix-based flow and transformation model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (5 × 8) − (1 × 2). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Solve invertible systems and distinguish existence from numerical stability.
